learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge on intermediate/high-intermediate-level of Korean grammar, structures and vocabulary.

Write essays/short paragraphs on various topics (e.g., reporting one's past experiences, describing illnesses and injuries, and writing about cultural differences) using appropriate vocabulary, grammar, and register.
Comprehend varied types of intermediate/high-intermediate level written texts in Korean including real-world sources material.
Demonstrate resilience as a language leaner as they develop and deploy linguistically and culturally appropriate responses to a range of familiar social/cultural interactions.

assessments.due_date:
Assignment I - WEEK 5 - 28/08/2026 5:00 pm

Assignment II - WEEK 7 - 11/09/2026 5:00 pm

Assignment III - WEEK 9 - 25/09/2026 5:00 pm

Assignment IV - WEEK 11 - 16/10/2026 5:00 pm

Due 5pm, Friday of designated weeks.
assessments.task_description:
There will be 4 Written Assignments due throughout the semester at 5pm, Friday of designated weeks:

  • Written Assignment I - WEEK 5
  • Written Assignment II - WEEK 7
  • Written Assignment III - WEEK 9
  • Written Assignment IV - WEEK 11

Assignment guidelines will be provided two weeks before each assignment due date.

Guidelines for Using Generative AI in Assignments

  • The assignment coversheet must be submitted as part of this assignment, whether generative AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Google Gemini) have been used or not.
  • A coversheet template will be provided.
  • If generative AI tools have been used in the assignment, students are required to submit both the original draft and the version processed using generative AI tools.
  • Any use of generative AI must be acknowledged in the assessment submission. Details of how generative AI has been used are indicated on the assignment coversheet template.
  • For further details and an example coversheet, please refer to this link: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/generative-ai-tools-assignments.

Gen AI & Machine Translation (MT) statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
12/10/2026

The quiz will take place in the first class of WEEK 11.
assessments.task_description:
Students will complete the quiz during the first tutorial in WEEK 11 and will have 50 minutes to complete it.

The quiz will mainly require students to provide the English or Korean equivalent of a given word or expression (for example, 사과 – apple) and will also include multiple-choice questions. It is designed primarily to assess students’ knowledge of the vocabulary and expressions covered up to and including Week 10.

Further guidelines for the quiz will be provided in Week 8.

STATEMENT ON AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

  • Bring your UQ student ID.
